purpose: Canonical, model-independent framework for language processing in Projekt Kontinuum.
principle: CLPF processes language into semantic representations; CCP-Cognitive processes meaning in the cognitive workflow.

processing_steps.name: Input Acquisition
processing_steps.purpose: Acquire text, transcribed speech, file content and structured data.

processing_steps.name: Normalization
processing_steps.purpose: Normalize Unicode, character sets, language, locale, formatting, sentence boundaries and document boundaries.

processing_steps.name: Tokenization
processing_steps.purpose: Create canonical token objects independent of tokenizer family.

processing_steps.name: Embedding Preparation
processing_steps.purpose: Prepare token, position, segment and context information for interchangeable model processing.

processing_steps.name: Transformer Processing
processing_steps.purpose: Conceptually place transformer processing as one interchangeable language representation mechanism.

processing_steps.name: Semantic Representation
processing_steps.purpose: Produce a model-independent semantic representation for CLU, CCP-Cognitive, CRE, Learning, Memory and Meta-Reasoning.

relationships.clu: CLU uses CLPF output for intent, context and meaning tasks.
relationships.ccp_cognitive: CCP-Cognitive processes meaning in a controlled cognitive workflow.
relationships.cre: CRE receives structured capability-relevant signals, not raw language.
relationships.chif: CHIF governs human interaction; CLPF prepares language input.
relationships.cmlf: CMLF governs learning media; CLPF does not select media.
relationships.cvf: CVF remains computer vision; CLPF remains language processing.
relationships.memory: Memory stores only validated, approved context and not unchecked raw language.
relationships.meta_reasoning: Meta-Reasoning can inspect assumptions and representation uncertainty.
relationships.release_integrity: Release Integrity validates later CLPF artifacts when implementation is approved.

recommendation: GO for conceptual canonical preparation; SPAETER for technical implementation.
